title: Decrease-and-Conquer Quiz
instructions: Answer each question. MCQs are auto-graded. Short answers are graded by Claude (default) or self-grading.

options: Splitting into many subproblems and combining all
Reducing to one smaller instance of the same problem
Finding shortest paths in weighted graphs
Finding shortest paths by number of edges in unweighted graphs
Always using less memory than DFS
Producing topological order for every directed graph

questions.prompt: Binary search is an example of:
BST operations are $O(h)$, where $h$ is tree height. In the worst case (skewed tree), this is:
In one or two sentences, explain one key difference between DFS and BFS.
questions.expected: DFS goes deep before backtracking; BFS explores level by level from the source.

questions.prompt: Give one valid topological ordering for this DAG: $A \to C$, $B \to C$, $C \to D$.
questions.expected: Example: $A, B, C, D$ (also $B, A, C, D$ works).

questions.prompt: Use Euclid's algorithm to compute $\gcd(48, 18)$. Show the remainder steps.
Why is insertion sort often fast on almost-sorted data?
questions.expected: Because most elements are already near correct positions, the inner shift loop does very little work on each pass.
